title: Soft Delete Salesforce Fields Removed from Org and Track Deletion History
story: As a DataTools Pro user managing my Data Dictionary, I want fields that have been deleted in Salesforce to be automatically hidden from the dictionary table while retaining a history log of the deletion, so that my dictionary stays clean without losing audit trail information.
description:
When Salesforce fields are deleted from an org, they should no longer appear in the DataTools Pro Data Dictionary table. However, the deletion should be tracked for audit purposes.

## Behavior
1. During a metadata refresh, the system compares local fields against the Salesforce response
2. Fields present locally but missing from Salesforce are soft-deleted (flagged, not physically removed)
3. The deletion date is recorded as the last status update for that field
4. Soft-deleted fields are filtered out of the dictionary data table
5. The change history log records a "Field Deleted" entry for each removed field

## History Tracking
New fields added to Salesforce are logged as "New Field" in the change history. Fields removed from Salesforce are logged as "Field Deleted" with a timestamp.
acceptance_criteria: Fields deleted from Salesforce are soft-deleted in the local database on next refresh
Soft-deleted fields are hidden from the dictionary data table
A 'salesforce_deleted' flag is set to 'yes' in the database for deleted fields
The deletion date is recorded as the last status update timestamp
Change history shows a 'Field Deleted' log entry for each removed field
Change history shows a 'New Field' log entry when new fields are detected
Soft-deleted fields can be reviewed in the change history log
ux_changes: Deleted Salesforce fields are no longer displayed in the dictionary data table. Change history log shows 'New Field' and 'Field Deleted' entries with timestamps.
middleware_changes: Metadata refresh logic now compares local fields against Salesforce response and soft-deletes fields that are no longer present. Change history entries are created for new and deleted fields.
data_model_changes: Added 'salesforce_deleted' flag column to the fields table. Soft delete mechanism records the deletion date as the last status update timestamp.
implementation_notes: Tested by adding a custom field ('Test Change History') to the Account object in Salesforce, refreshing in DTP to see 'New Field' log, then deleting the field and refreshing again to see 'Field Deleted' log. The field is marked with salesforce_deleted=yes in the database and filtered from the data table.
comment_summary: Waqar demonstrated the feature by creating and deleting a test field in Salesforce. Change history correctly logged both 'New Field' and 'Field Deleted' events. The field was removed from the data table and marked as deleted in the database.
